title: MLS Commit arrives before Welcome: garbage displayed on join
description: Trigger: Admin adds B via add_member(), broadcasts Commit, then whispers Welcome to B. B receives the Commit (as a Deliver broadcast) before the Welcome arrives (as WhisperDeliver). At the time B receives the Commit, mls_active=false. The code path (commands.rs:303-304) does payload.clone() -- the raw TLS-serialized MLS Commit bytes -- and then prints them via String::from_utf8_lossy as the message body. B sees unreadable binary garbage attributed to the admin's pub_id in their terminal, then the Welcome arrives and mls_active=true for subsequent messages. This is not a security issue but is confusing UX with no indication to B that a protocol join is in progress. The fix is to buffer or silently drop pre-join Deliver messages when mls_active=false.
